item.title: What’s up with Netanyahu? I’m answering your questions.
item.description: David’s live chat with readers started at 12 p.m. ET on Monday. Submit your questions now.

item.title: Don’t trust this $4 solution for getting a prescription
item.description: Should AI prescribe your meds? The evidence is lacking.

item.title: There are two winners in Iran. Neither one is America.
item.description: Oil disruption benefits Russia, as does less U.S. aid for Ukraine. And Iran distracts from China.

item.title: Congress is done waiting for Big Tech to protect kids online
item.description: Parents and their children deserve laws that work to reduce social media harms.

item.title: China may be preparing for nuclear war. Trump can’t ignore it.
item.description: Rather than aiming for nuclear deterrence, China appears to be building a stockpile to wage war.

item.title: Trump is trying something new in Iran. Hold on tight.
item.description: Critics demanding a “day after” plan are confusing this presidency with that of George W. Bush.

item.title: Your salted caramel mocha latte is destroying society
item.description: Edmund Burke would have had thoughts about the consumption of that vanilla sweet cream nitro cold brew.

item.title: Israeli officials are growing concerned
item.description: A bombing campaign nearing its military goals in Iran leaves the hardest questions unanswered.

item.title: Legislators think they’re making trains safer. Evidence says otherwise.
item.description: The Railway Safety Act would impose costly mandates with no proven links to safety.

item.title: The insidious danger of scandal fatigue
item.description: Rep. Anthony Weiner‘s press secretary on scandal saturation, plus readers on AI, home school, ‘God Gap’ and more.

item.title: Cracking down on graduation speeches won’t solve the problem on campus
item.description: Universities need to do a better job of preparing students to engage civilly in the real world.

item.title: The FCC thinks it knows best
item.description: The central planners running the obsolete agency have another plan to meddle in the private economy.

item.title: Your social media ‘addiction’ is overrated
item.description: It is healthier and more accurate not to pathologize our habits.

item.title: The myth of the billionaire wealth tax
item.description: The proposal from Bernie Sanders exposes the difference between paper wealth and real resources.

item.title: California’s false choice on autonomous trucks
item.description: Gavin Newsom’s successor could put the state on autopilot to economic irrelevance.

item.title: Can the military and AI firms get along?
item.description: The whole country suffers when government needlessly punishes innovative firms.

item.title: The war that would make Iran look mild
item.description: Delaying arms shipments to Taiwan undermines deterrence at a dangerous time.
